<div class="min-w-0 isolate" role="main">

<div class="ps-0">

<div>

<div class="px-5 sm:px-12 pt-3.5">

<div class="max-w-4xl ms-0 2xl:mx-auto">

<a href="https://react.dev/reference/react" class="text-link dark:text-link-dark text-sm tracking-wide font-bold uppercase me-1 hover:underline">API Reference</a><span class="inline-block me-1 text-link dark:text-link-dark text-lg rtl:rotate-180">![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)</span>

# createElement<a href="https://react.dev/reference/react/createElement#undefined" class="mdx-header-anchor hidden" aria-label="Link for this heading" title="Link for this heading"><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="text-gray-70 ms-2 h-5 w-5" /></a>

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="px-5 sm:px-12">

<div class="max-w-7xl mx-auto">

<div class="max-w-4xl ms-0 2xl:mx-auto">

<div class="font-display text-xl text-primary dark:text-primary-dark leading-relaxed">

`createElement` lets you create a React element. It serves as an alternative to writing <a href="https://react.dev/learn/writing-markup-with-jsx" class="inline text-link dark:text-link-dark border-b border-link border-opacity-0 hover:border-opacity-100 duration-100 ease-in transition leading-normal">JSX.</a>

<div class="sandpack sandpack--codeblock rounded-2xl h-full w-full overflow-x-auto flex items-center bg-wash dark:bg-gray-95 shadow-lg my-8" dir="ltr" style="contain:content">

<div class="sp-wrapper">

<div class="sp-stack">

<div class="sp-code-editor">

``` sp-cm
const element = createElement(type, props, ...children)
```

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>

- <a href="https://react.dev/reference/react/createElement#reference" class="inline text-link dark:text-link-dark border-b border-link border-opacity-0 hover:border-opacity-100 duration-100 ease-in transition leading-normal">Reference</a>
  - <a href="https://react.dev/reference/react/createElement#createelement" class="inline text-link dark:text-link-dark border-b border-link border-opacity-0 hover:border-opacity-100 duration-100 ease-in transition leading-normal"><code class="inline text-code text-secondary dark:text-secondary-dark px-1 rounded-md no-underline bg-gray-30 bg-opacity-10 py-px" dir="ltr">createElement(type, props, ...children)</code></a>
- <a href="https://react.dev/reference/react/createElement#usage" class="inline text-link dark:text-link-dark border-b border-link border-opacity-0 hover:border-opacity-100 duration-100 ease-in transition leading-normal">Usage</a>
  - <a href="https://react.dev/reference/react/createElement#creating-an-element-without-jsx" class="inline text-link dark:text-link-dark border-b border-link border-opacity-0 hover:border-opacity-100 duration-100 ease-in transition leading-normal">Creating an element without JSX</a>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Reference <a href="https://react.dev/reference/react/createElement#reference" class="mdx-header-anchor inline-block" aria-label="Link for Reference " title="Link for Reference "><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="text-gray-70 ms-2 h-5 w-5" /></a>

### `createElement(type, props, ...children)` <a href="https://react.dev/reference/react/createElement#createelement" class="mdx-header-anchor inline-block" aria-label="Link for this heading" title="Link for this heading"><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="text-gray-70 ms-2 h-5 w-5" /></a>

Call `createElement` to create a React element with the given `type`, `props`, and `children`.

<div class="sandpack sandpack--codeblock rounded-2xl h-full w-full overflow-x-auto flex items-center bg-wash dark:bg-gray-95 shadow-lg my-8" dir="ltr" style="contain:content">

<div class="sp-wrapper">

<div class="sp-stack">

<div class="sp-code-editor">

``` sp-cm
import { createElement } from 'react';

function Greeting({ name }) {
  return createElement(
    'h1',
    { className: 'greeting' },
    'Hello'
  );
}
```

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>

<a href="https://react.dev/reference/react/createElement#usage" class="inline text-link dark:text-link-dark border-b border-link border-opacity-0 hover:border-opacity-100 duration-100 ease-in transition leading-normal">See more examples below.</a>

#### Parameters <a href="https://react.dev/reference/react/createElement#parameters" class="mdx-header-anchor inline-block" aria-label="Link for Parameters " title="Link for Parameters "><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="text-gray-70 ms-2 h-5 w-5" /></a>

- `type`: The `type` argument must be a valid React component type. For example, it could be a tag name string (such as `'div'` or `'span'`), or a React component (a function, a class, or a special component like <a href="https://react.dev/reference/react/Fragment" class="inline text-link dark:text-link-dark border-b border-link border-opacity-0 hover:border-opacity-100 duration-100 ease-in transition leading-normal"><code class="inline text-code text-secondary dark:text-secondary-dark px-1 rounded-md no-underline bg-gray-30 bg-opacity-10 py-px" dir="ltr">Fragment</code></a>).

- `props`: The `props` argument must either be an object or `null`. If you pass `null`, it will be treated the same as an empty object. React will create an element with props matching the `props` you have passed. Note that `ref` and `key` from your `props` object are special and will *not* be available as `element.props.ref` and `element.props.key` on the returned `element`. They will be available as `element.ref` and `element.key`.

- **optional** `...children`: Zero or more child nodes. They can be any React nodes, including React elements, strings, numbers, <a href="https://react.dev/reference/react-dom/createPortal" class="inline text-link dark:text-link-dark border-b border-link border-opacity-0 hover:border-opacity-100 duration-100 ease-in transition leading-normal">portals</a>, empty nodes (`null`, `undefined`, `true`, and `false`), and arrays of React nodes.

#### Returns <a href="https://react.dev/reference/react/createElement#returns" class="mdx-header-anchor inline-block" aria-label="Link for Returns " title="Link for Returns "><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="text-gray-70 ms-2 h-5 w-5" /></a>

`createElement` returns a React element object with a few properties:

- `type`: The `type` you have passed.
- `props`: The `props` you have passed except for `ref` and `key`.
- `ref`: The `ref` you have passed. If missing, `null`.
- `key`: The `key` you have passed, coerced to a string. If missing, `null`.

Usually, you’ll return the element from your component or make it a child of another element. Although you may read the element’s properties, it’s best to treat every element as opaque after it’s created, and only render it.

#### Caveats <a href="https://react.dev/reference/react/createElement#caveats" class="mdx-header-anchor inline-block" aria-label="Link for Caveats " title="Link for Caveats "><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="text-gray-70 ms-2 h-5 w-5" /></a>

- You must **treat React elements and their props as <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Immutable_object" class="inline text-link dark:text-link-dark border-b border-link border-opacity-0 hover:border-opacity-100 duration-100 ease-in transition leading-normal" target="_blank" rel="nofollow noopener noreferrer">immutable</a>** and never change their contents after creation. In development, React will <a href="https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Object/freeze" class="inline text-link dark:text-link-dark border-b border-link border-opacity-0 hover:border-opacity-100 duration-100 ease-in transition leading-normal" target="_blank" rel="nofollow noopener noreferrer">freeze</a> the returned element and its `props` property shallowly to enforce this.

- When you use JSX, **you must start a tag with a capital letter to render your own custom component.** In other words, `<Something />` is equivalent to `createElement(Something)`, but `<something />` (lowercase) is equivalent to `createElement('something')` (note it’s a string, so it will be treated as a built-in HTML tag).

- You should only **pass children as multiple arguments to `createElement` if they are all statically known,** like `createElement('h1', {}, child1, child2, child3)`. If your children are dynamic, pass the entire array as the third argument: `createElement('ul', {}, listItems)`. This ensures that React will <a href="https://react.dev/learn/rendering-lists#keeping-list-items-in-order-with-key" class="inline text-link dark:text-link-dark border-b border-link border-opacity-0 hover:border-opacity-100 duration-100 ease-in transition leading-normal">warn you about missing <code class="inline text-code text-secondary dark:text-secondary-dark px-1 rounded-md no-underline bg-gray-30 bg-opacity-10 py-px" dir="ltr">key</code>s</a> for any dynamic lists. For static lists this is not necessary because they never reorder.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Usage <a href="https://react.dev/reference/react/createElement#usage" class="mdx-header-anchor inline-block" aria-label="Link for Usage " title="Link for Usage "><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="text-gray-70 ms-2 h-5 w-5" /></a>

### Creating an element without JSX <a href="https://react.dev/reference/react/createElement#creating-an-element-without-jsx" class="mdx-header-anchor inline-block" aria-label="Link for Creating an element without JSX " title="Link for Creating an element without JSX "><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="text-gray-70 ms-2 h-5 w-5" /></a>

If you don’t like <a href="https://react.dev/learn/writing-markup-with-jsx" class="inline text-link dark:text-link-dark border-b border-link border-opacity-0 hover:border-opacity-100 duration-100 ease-in transition leading-normal">JSX</a> or can’t use it in your project, you can use `createElement` as an alternative.

To create an element without JSX, call `createElement` with some <span class="code-step bg-opacity-10 dark:bg-opacity-20 relative rounded px-[6px] py-[1.5px] border-b-[2px] border-opacity-60 bg-blue-40 border-blue-40 text-blue-60 dark:text-blue-30" data-step="1">type</span>, <span class="code-step bg-opacity-10 dark:bg-opacity-20 relative rounded px-[6px] py-[1.5px] border-b-[2px] border-opacity-60 bg-yellow-40 border-yellow-40 text-yellow-60 dark:text-yellow-30" data-step="2">props</span>, and <span class="code-step bg-opacity-10 dark:bg-opacity-20 relative rounded px-[6px] py-[1.5px] border-b-[2px] border-opacity-60 bg-purple-40 border-purple-40 text-purple-60 dark:text-purple-30" data-step="3">children</span>:

<div class="sandpack sandpack--codeblock rounded-2xl h-full w-full overflow-x-auto flex items-center bg-wash dark:bg-gray-95 shadow-lg my-8" dir="ltr" style="contain:content">

<div class="sp-wrapper">

<div class="sp-stack">

<div class="sp-code-editor">

``` sp-cm
import { createElement } from 'react';

function Greeting({ name }) {
  return createElement(
    'h1',
    { className: 'greeting' },
    'Hello ',
    createElement('i', null, name),
    '. Welcome!'
  );
}
```

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>

The <span class="code-step bg-opacity-10 dark:bg-opacity-20 relative rounded px-[6px] py-[1.5px] border-b-[2px] border-opacity-60 bg-purple-40 border-purple-40 text-purple-60 dark:text-purple-30" data-step="3">children</span> are optional, and you can pass as many as you need (the example above has three children). This code will display a `<h1>` header with a greeting. For comparison, here is the same example rewritten with JSX:

<div class="sandpack sandpack--codeblock rounded-2xl h-full w-full overflow-x-auto flex items-center bg-wash dark:bg-gray-95 shadow-lg my-8" dir="ltr" style="contain:content">

<div class="sp-wrapper">

<div class="sp-stack">

<div class="sp-code-editor">

``` sp-cm
function Greeting({ name }) {
  return (
    <h1 className="greeting">
      Hello <i>{name}</i>. Welcome!
    </h1>
  );
}
```

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>

To render your own React component, pass a function like `Greeting` as the <span class="code-step bg-opacity-10 dark:bg-opacity-20 relative rounded px-[6px] py-[1.5px] border-b-[2px] border-opacity-60 bg-blue-40 border-blue-40 text-blue-60 dark:text-blue-30" data-step="1">type</span> instead of a string like `'h1'`:

<div class="sandpack sandpack--codeblock rounded-2xl h-full w-full overflow-x-auto flex items-center bg-wash dark:bg-gray-95 shadow-lg my-8" dir="ltr" style="contain:content">

<div class="sp-wrapper">

<div class="sp-stack">

<div class="sp-code-editor">

``` sp-cm
export default function App() {
  return createElement(Greeting, { name: 'Taylor' });
}
```

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>

With JSX, it would look like this:

<div class="sandpack sandpack--codeblock rounded-2xl h-full w-full overflow-x-auto flex items-center bg-wash dark:bg-gray-95 shadow-lg my-8" dir="ltr" style="contain:content">

<div class="sp-wrapper">

<div class="sp-stack">

<div class="sp-code-editor">

``` sp-cm
export default function App() {
  return <Greeting name="Taylor" />;
}
```

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>

Here is a complete example written with `createElement`:

</div>

<div class="sandpack sandpack--playground w-full my-8" dir="ltr">

<div class="sp-wrapper">

<div class="shadow-lg dark:shadow-lg-dark rounded-lg" style="contain:content">

<div class="bg-wash dark:bg-card-dark flex justify-between items-center relative z-10 border-b border-border dark:border-border-dark rounded-t-lg text-lg">

<div class="flex-1 grow min-w-0 px-4 lg:px-6">

<div>

<div class="relative overflow-hidden">

<div class="w-[fit-content] invisible">

<div class="sp-tabs" translate="no">

<div class="sp-tabs-scrollable-container" aria-label="Select active file" role="tablist">

App.js

</div>

</div>

</div>

<span class="h-full py-2 px-1 mt-px -mb-px flex border-b text-link dark:text-link-dark border-link dark:border-link-dark items-center text-md leading-tight truncate" style="max-width:160px">App.js</span>

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="px-3 flex items-center justify-end text-start" translate="yes">

<img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="inline mx-1 relative" /><span class="hidden md:block">Reload</span>

<img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="inline mx-1 relative" /><span class="hidden md:block">Clear</span>

<a href="https://codesandbox.io/api/v1/sandboxes/define?undefined&amp;environment=create-react-app" class="text-sm text-primary dark:text-primary-dark inline-flex items-center hover:text-link duration-100 ease-in transition mx-1 ms-2 md:ms-1" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank" title="Open in CodeSandbox"><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="inline mx-1 relative top-[1px]" /><span class="hidden md:block">Fork</span></a>

</div>

</div>

<div class="sp-layout">

<div class="sp-editor sp-stack">

<div class="sp-code-editor">

<div class="sp-pristine sp-javascript sp-cm" aria-autocomplete="list" aria-label="Code Editor for App.js" aria-multiline="true" role="textbox" tabindex="0" translate="no">

``` sp-pre-placeholder
import { createElement } from 'react';

function Greeting({ name }) {
  return createElement(
    'h1',
    { className: 'greeting' },
    'Hello ',
    createElement('i', null, name),
    '. Welcome!'
  );
}

export default function App() {
  return createElement(
    Greeting,
    { name: 'Taylor' }
  );
}
```

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="order-last xl:order-2 sp-stack">

<div class="p-0 sm:p-2 md:p-4 lg:p-8 bg-card dark:bg-wash-dark h-full relative md:rounded-b-lg lg:rounded-b-none">

<div style="position:relative">

</div>

</div>

</div>

<span class="flex p-2 focus:outline-none text-primary dark:text-primary-dark leading-[20px]"><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="rotate-0 inline me-1.5 text-xl" />Show more</span>

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="max-w-4xl ms-0 2xl:mx-auto">

And here is the same example written using JSX:

</div>

<div class="sandpack sandpack--playground w-full my-8" dir="ltr">

<div class="sp-wrapper">

<div class="shadow-lg dark:shadow-lg-dark rounded-lg" style="contain:content">

<div class="bg-wash dark:bg-card-dark flex justify-between items-center relative z-10 border-b border-border dark:border-border-dark rounded-t-lg text-lg">

<div class="flex-1 grow min-w-0 px-4 lg:px-6">

<div>

<div class="relative overflow-hidden">

<div class="w-[fit-content] invisible">

<div class="sp-tabs" translate="no">

<div class="sp-tabs-scrollable-container" aria-label="Select active file" role="tablist">

App.js

</div>

</div>

</div>

<span class="h-full py-2 px-1 mt-px -mb-px flex border-b text-link dark:text-link-dark border-link dark:border-link-dark items-center text-md leading-tight truncate" style="max-width:160px">App.js</span>

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="px-3 flex items-center justify-end text-start" translate="yes">

<img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="inline mx-1 relative" /><span class="hidden md:block">Reload</span>

<img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="inline mx-1 relative" /><span class="hidden md:block">Clear</span>

<a href="https://codesandbox.io/api/v1/sandboxes/define?undefined&amp;environment=create-react-app" class="text-sm text-primary dark:text-primary-dark inline-flex items-center hover:text-link duration-100 ease-in transition mx-1 ms-2 md:ms-1" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank" title="Open in CodeSandbox"><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="inline mx-1 relative top-[1px]" /><span class="hidden md:block">Fork</span></a>

</div>

</div>

<div class="rounded-b-lg overflow-hidden sp-layout">

<div class="sp-editor sp-stack">

<div class="sp-code-editor">

<div class="sp-pristine sp-javascript sp-cm" aria-autocomplete="list" aria-label="Code Editor for App.js" aria-multiline="true" role="textbox" tabindex="0" translate="no">

``` sp-pre-placeholder
function Greeting({ name }) {
  return (
    <h1 className="greeting">
      Hello <i>{name}</i>. Welcome!
    </h1>
  );
}

export default function App() {
  return <Greeting name="Taylor" />;
}
```

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="order-last xl:order-2 sp-stack">

<div class="p-0 sm:p-2 md:p-4 lg:p-8 bg-card dark:bg-wash-dark h-full relative md:rounded-b-lg lg:rounded-b-none">

<div style="position:relative">

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="max-w-4xl ms-0 2xl:mx-auto">

Both coding styles are fine, so you can use whichever one you prefer for your project. The main benefit of using JSX compared to `createElement` is that it’s easy to see which closing tag corresponds to which opening tag.

##### <img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="inline me-2 dark:text-purple-30 text-purple-40" />Deep Dive

<div class="mb-4">

#### What is a React element, exactly? <a href="https://react.dev/reference/react/createElement#what-is-a-react-element-exactly" class="mdx-header-anchor inline-block" aria-label="Link for What is a React element, exactly? " title="Link for What is a React element, exactly? "><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="text-gray-70 ms-2 h-5 w-5" /></a>

</div>

<span class="me-1"><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="rotate-0" /></span>Show Details

<div class="p-8 border-t dark:border-purple-60 border-purple-10">

An element is a lightweight description of a piece of the user interface. For example, both `<Greeting name="Taylor" />` and `createElement(Greeting, { name: 'Taylor' })` produce an object like this:

<div class="sandpack sandpack--codeblock rounded-2xl h-full w-full overflow-x-auto flex items-center bg-wash dark:bg-gray-95 shadow-lg my-8" dir="ltr" style="contain:content">

<div class="sp-wrapper">

<div class="sp-stack">

<div class="sp-code-editor">

``` sp-cm
// Slightly simplified
{
  type: Greeting,
  props: {
    name: 'Taylor'
  },
  key: null,
  ref: null,
}
```

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>

**Note that creating this object does not render the `Greeting` component or create any DOM elements.**

A React element is more like a description—an instruction for React to later render the `Greeting` component. By returning this object from your `App` component, you tell React what to do next.

Creating elements is extremely cheap so you don’t need to try to optimize or avoid it.

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="grid grid-cols-1 gap-4 py-4 mx-auto max-w-7xl md:grid-cols-2 md:py-12">

<a href="https://react.dev/reference/react/Component" class="flex gap-x-4 md:gap-x-6 items-center w-full md:min-w-80 md:w-fit md:max-w-md px-4 md:px-5 py-6 border-2 border-transparent text-base leading-base text-link dark:text-link-dark rounded-lg group focus:text-link dark:focus:text-link-dark focus:bg-highlight focus:border-link dark:focus:bg-highlight-dark dark:focus:border-link-dark focus:border-opacity-100 focus:border-2 focus:ring-1 focus:ring-offset-4 focus:ring-blue-40 active:ring-0 active:ring-offset-0 hover:bg-gray-5 dark:hover:bg-gray-80"><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="duration-100 ease-in transition rotate-90 rtl:-rotate-90 inline text-tertiary dark:text-tertiary-dark group-focus:text-link dark:group-focus:text-link-dark" /></a>

<div class="flex flex-col overflow-hidden">

<span class="text-sm font-bold tracking-wide no-underline uppercase text-secondary dark:text-secondary-dark group-focus:text-link dark:group-focus:text-link-dark group-focus:text-opacity-100">Previous</span><span class="text-lg break-words group-hover:underline">Component</span>

</div>

<a href="https://react.dev/reference/react/createRef" class="flex gap-x-4 md:gap-x-6 items-center w-full md:min-w-80 md:w-fit md:max-w-md px-4 md:px-5 py-6 border-2 border-transparent text-base leading-base text-link dark:text-link-dark rounded-lg group focus:text-link dark:focus:text-link-dark focus:bg-highlight focus:border-link dark:focus:bg-highlight-dark dark:focus:border-link-dark focus:border-opacity-100 focus:border-2 focus:ring-1 focus:ring-offset-4 focus:ring-blue-40 active:ring-0 active:ring-offset-0 hover:bg-gray-5 dark:hover:bg-gray-80 flex-row-reverse justify-self-end text-end"><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="duration-100 ease-in transition -rotate-90 rtl:rotate-90 inline text-tertiary dark:text-tertiary-dark group-focus:text-link dark:group-focus:text-link-dark" /></a>

<div class="flex flex-col overflow-hidden">

<span class="text-sm font-bold tracking-wide no-underline uppercase text-secondary dark:text-secondary-dark group-focus:text-link dark:group-focus:text-link-dark group-focus:text-opacity-100">Next</span><span class="text-lg break-words group-hover:underline">createRef</span>

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="w-full px-5 pt-10 mx-auto sm:px-12 md:px-12 md:pt-12 lg:pt-10">

## On this page

<div class="h-full overflow-y-auto ps-4 max-h-[calc(100vh-7.5rem)]" style="overscroll-behavior:contain">
